title: Calverton Fairey Battle L5499 Memorial
cause_of_loss: Lost in fog and blackout conditions returning from France
narrative: The earliest crash in the Farnsfield area and the only one involving a Fairey Battle — a light bomber already obsolete by 1940. 300 Squadron was Poland's first bomber squadron in exile, formed from Polish airmen who had escaped occupied Poland to continue fighting from Britain. Returning from bombing Calais harbour, fog rolled across Nottinghamshire. F/O Gebicki, an experienced 39-year-old pilot, stayed airborne until fuel was nearly exhausted, then attempted to land in darkness. The memorial — a steel cross with the inscription 'Perished for our and your freedom' — was erected by coal miners building the railway to Calverton Colliery. In 2019, Gebicki's son, Professor Janusz Gebicki, flew from Australia to attend a memorial service.
inscription: Perished for our and your freedom
erected_by: Coal miners building Calverton Colliery railway
